differential_diagnoses: Severe insulin resistance syndrome (e.g., insulin receptor mutation or autoantibodies)
next_steps: Measure insulin antibody levels and anti-insulin receptor antibodies
Re-evaluate insulin administration technique and monitor glucose in a controlled setting to rule out factitious non-adherence
Assess for insulin amyloidosis at injection sites via physical exam and/or imaging
Evaluate for underlying autoimmune disorders (e.g., lupus-like syndrome)
Consider discontinuation of high-dose exogenous insulin in favor of alternative therapies like leptin replacement (if candidate) or U-500 insulin with intensive monitoring
Consult endocrinology, dermatology, and potentially immunology for multidisciplinary management

differential_diagnoses: Type B insulin resistance (insulin receptor autoantibodies)
Severe insulin resistance due to subcutaneous insulin sequestration (insulin-derived amyloidosis)
Paraneoplastic syndrome associated with cachexia and autoimmunity
next_steps: Measure serum insulin and C-peptide levels simultaneously during a hyperglycemic episode to differentiate between true resistance and factitious disorder
Perform diagnostic testing for anti-insulin receptor antibodies (Type B insulin resistance screen)
Conduct observed inpatient insulin administration (supervised monitoring) to strictly rule out non-adherence
Evaluate physical injection sites with ultrasound or biopsy to assess for deep-tissue lipohypertrophy or insulin-derived amyloidosis
Comprehensive evaluation for underlying malignancy (e.g., PET-CT) given progressive weight loss and anemia/leukopenia
Characterize positive ANA with specific ENA panel and repeat complement studies
